eldora.note: 9:28 AM update: Zero traffic, tons of parking, soft snow, and beach vibes all day! Get on up here! And don't miss Earl Nelson and The Company playing their signature brand of Colorado country blues outside on Timbers Deck from 12:00 to 3:00. -- 5:39 AM update: Good morning, skiers and riders! Welcome to the Eldora snow report for Saturday, March 21, 2026. It's gonna be a beach party up here today! If you're into soft snow, slush bumps, and spring vibes—and let's be honest, who isn't—Eldora is the place you want to be. We are NOT expecting a busy day, so parking should be plentiful, and slopes and lift mazes should be empty. We're looking at a HOT one—71 degrees—with a westerly breeze to take the edge off. Break out the Zinka and your loudest beachwear and come join us for some spring laps! Activities today include Ski with a Ranger (meet our Forest Service friends at 11:00 AM at the top of Alpenglow), plus live tunes in the base area all day: Yololo spins from 9:00 to 12:00 at the Woodward BoomBox and Earl Nelson and The Company play Timbers Deck from 12:00 to 3:00. 2026-27 season passes are now on sale . Lock in your lowest price now, before prices increase on April 30. Reminder: Spring Hours are now in effect through Closing Day. Lift Hours: 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM, seven days a week Lodge Hours: 8:00 AM to 4:30 PM, seven days a week NWS Weather Snapshot: Sunny, with a high near 71. Windy, with a west wind 15 to 25 mph, with gusts as high as 38 mph. Uphill and Nordic Skiing: Uphill access is open, subject to the policies described at eldora.com/uphill . The Nordic Center is open 9:00 to 12:30 for Nordic Season Pass Holders and Nordic 3-Pass guests; day-ticket access and snowshoeing are not available. Check Nordic Pulse for the latest updates. Stay in the loop: This page, eldora.com/conditions , is always your go-to for the latest updates, but you can also sign up for text alerts so you never miss a beat. Text one of the following keywords to (866) 965-6224: ELDORA for general alerts, UPHILL for uphill-specific updates, or NORDIC for Nordic updates. Message and data rates may apply. Thanks for checking the Eldora snow report. We'll see you on the hill! -- GENERAL INFORMATION Lift Hours: 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM, seven days a week. Lodge Hours: 8:00 AM to 4:30 PM, seven days a week. Single-Occupancy Vehicle (SOV) Fees: Vehicles with two or more occupants may park for free seven days a week. Vehicles with one occupant will be charged $10 on weekends, during certain holiday periods, and on weekdays with 10 or more inches of new snow. Credit/debit is accepted; cash is not. High-Occupancy Vehicle (HOV) Parking: Per Boulder County requirements, the Elk Lot (nearest to Alpenglow chair) is reserved seven days a week for vehicles with three or more occupants on a first-come, first-served basis. Learn more here . EV Parking: Eldora provides 30 free ChargePoint EV charging stations. These premium parking spots are available on a first-come, first-served basis and are subject to the SOV policy above. To park in the EV lot, you MUST have an EV and be actively charging. If you are finished charging, please move your car to another non-EV space so others can use the chargers. Easy, Convenient RTD Bus Service from Boulder: RTD’s NB route brings you from Downtown Boulder Station straight to the Alpenglow chairlift in about 40 minutes; riders 19 and under ride RTD for FREE through RTD's Zero Fare for Youth program. For schedule and fare info, visit rtd-denver.com

killington.note: Evenin’, Killington, We were dealt some firmer surfaces today, but the sun showed up to play and made for a pretty sweet one at The Beast. We could be looking at a lot of snow or a lot of nothing depending on how Mother Nature is feeling. Between tonight and tomorrow, we could see 8 inches of snow or just a dusting. With forecasts providing a range of outcomes, I think we’ll just have to wait and see on this one. Tomorrow brings a high of 37 at the base and 34 near the peak, with strong winds from the south around 30 to 40 mph. It was hardback skiing and riding out there today, but we could see a big change in surface conditions. Even if we don’t get dumped on, temps should rise above freezing around midday which should soften surfaces up. If we don’t get warmer temps, we might get some snow instead. Either way, our operations team will be doing their thing so we can make the most of it. Our grooming team will be adding Skyelark to the plan as well as some steeps in Downdraft plus mid and lower Ovation. Some more mellow cruisers that will be smoothed over include Skyeburst, Pipe Dream, and Header. Head to our Lifts & Trails Report for the full rundown of groomed options. Our snowmaking team took advantage of the cold stretch earlier this week, firing on Snowshed Slope, Upper Skyelark, Lower Bittersweet, and Highlander, along with fan guns around K-1, Snowdon, and Mouse Trap. Stay tuned for more that may be in the works—we’re eyeing a window next week where we could fire up once again. If you haven’t yet, check out our latest Insider Blog to see how our recent snowmaking investments have been paying off. Our key lifts are scheduled to open up at 8:00 a.m., with the Canyon Quad and South Ridge Quad also joining the party at 9:00 a.m. Up in our kparks, Dream Maker is still firing with 16 features between the upper and lower sections, including our premier jump line and the sweet rail section near the base of Bear Mountain. The Stash is the place to be for a more natural feel, with plenty of creative stone and wooden features on the trail. Over on Ramshead, Timberline continues to shine with 44 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe, while Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard are all dialed in for fun progression options. Check our Terrain Parks Report for the lowdown on each feature. Lastly, if you can't get enough of spring at The Beast, grab a 2026 Spring Pass for unlimited skiing and riding through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade anytime for just $199. There’s more weekend to shred, get out there! - Evan Operating Day: 131 Lifts Key lifts operate seven days a week from 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Monday through Friday, and from 8:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Saturday and Sunday plus Peak Dates*; the Bear Mountain Quad will operate from 7:30 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. every Saturday through the end of March. The North Ridge Quad will open at 9:30 a.m. on weekdays and 8:00 a.m. on weekends, the Canyon Quad will spin from 10:00 a.m. to 3:30 p.m. Monday, Wednesday, Friday and 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on weekends, and the Sunrise Village Triple will spin until 3:00 p.m. on weekdays and 4:00 p.m. on weekends. A note for our snowboarders: Thanks to our new cabins on the Skyeship Gondola, we're asking you to please utilize the snowboard racks outside of each cabin. If the racks are entirely full, please put your boards underneath the seats. This differs from our K-1 Gondola, where all snowboards should be brought inside the cabins. Just remember: outside Skyeship, inside K-1! The Tubing Park presented by bubly remains open Friday - Sunday through March 22. Uphill Travel All uphill travel routes are currently open! Please visit killington.com/uphill for specific route information, including a map of each route. Before heading up the hill, be sure to review our uphill travel policies and grab your Uphill Travel Pass here . All uphill travelers are required to have an Uphill Travel Pass and armband, which can be picked up at any ticket window. Please keep an eye out for grooming equipment and steer clear of mountain ops during non-operating hours. MOUNTAIN SERVICES Parking & Shuttles Parking is available at the K-1, Snowshed, Ramshead, Bear, Skyeship and Vale parking lots. Please respect signage and allow our parking staff to assist you on your trip to the slopes. Shuttle service is available from 7:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. midweek and 6:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. on weekends and holidays. Winter car camping and overnight parking is only available in the lot across from Skyeship Lodge. See details here . Lodge Services All lodges are open daily from 8:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. on weekdays and 7:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. on weekends/peak dates*. K-1 Lodge Breakfast: Served starting at 8:30 a.m. on weekdays and 7:30 a.m. on weekends/peak dates. Lunch: Served from 10:30 a.m. - 3:00 p.m. daily. Brew Bar (2nd floor): Open serving handcrafted coffees at 8:30 a.m. on weekdays and at 7:30 a.m. on weekends, with cocktails and beers served starting at 9:00 a.m. K-1 Pub (3rd floor): Open at 10:00 a.m. daily. Rentals & Killington Sports at K-1: Open 30 minutes before lifts until 30 minutes after lifts. Guest Services: 1st floor - Open 30 minutes before lifts until 1:00 p.m. on weekdays and until 4:30 p.m. on weekends/peak dates. 2nd floor - Open 30 minutes before lifts until 30 minutes after lifts. Superstar Umbrella Bar: Open at 11:00 a.m. from Friday - Sunday offering beer, wine, liquor, and specialty hot cocktails. Snowshed Lodge Breakfast: Served starting at 8:30 a.m. on weekdays and 7:30 a.m. on weekends/peak dates. Lunch: Served from 10:30 a.m. - 3:30 p.m. daily. Long Trail Pub (3rd floor): Open daily from 10:30 a.m. - 6:00 p.m., offering our Lunch Dining Menu until 5:30 p.m. and lite fare until 6:00 p.m. Snowshed Umbrella Bar: Open on Saturdays and Sundays serving beers, cocktails, and light fare. Rentals, Killington Sports at Snowshed, & Guest Services: Open 30 minutes before lifts until 30 minutes after lifts. On Fridays, rentals & demos for the weekend can be picked up ahead of time between 2:00 p.m. and 6:00 p.m. Snowshed Demos & Tuning: Open 30 minutes before lifts until 30 minutes after lifts. Ski & snowboard demo packages available. Please note that binding mounts & adjustments are not offered at this time. Click here for more information. Private & Adult Group Lessons: Booked online in advance here . Ramshead Lodge Breakfast: Served starting at 8:30 a.m. on Fridays and 7:30 a.m. on weekends/peak dates. Lunch: Served daily with grab & go options & soups. The pizza station is open Thursday - Monday, and the full grill menu is available Friday - Sunday. Guest Services: Open 30 minutes before lifts until 12:00 p.m. on Monday – Thursday and until 4:30 p.m. on Friday - Sunday at the ticket windows outside of the lodge. Rentals: Open for youth lesson participants and families only. Youth Lessons: Youth Group Lessons and our Ministars program meet at Ramshead. Child Care Center: Open weekdays from 8:30 a.m. - 3:45 p.m. and weekends from 8:00 a.m. - 3:45 p.m. Bear Mountain Lodge Breakfast: Served starting at 8:30 a.m. on weekdays and 7:30 a.m. on weekends/peak dates. Lunch: Open daily with grab & go items and soups available. Full grill menu available Friday - Sunday. Bear Bar: Open at 10:00 a.m. until 4:30 p.m. on weekdays and until 5:00 p.m. on weekends. Guest Services: Available Friday - Monday at ticket windows outside the lodge. Open 30 minutes before lifts. Kiosk lift ticket pickup is available daily. Skyeship Lodge Food & Bar Service: Available daily from The Rollin’ Rooster. Guest Services: Open daily inside the lodge. Closing Date: March 29th On-Mountain Dining Options Peak Lodge – Open daily from 9:30 a.m. - 3:30 p.m. Grill open daily from 10:30 a.m. - 3:00 p.m. Peak Lodge Bar offers its own menu daily with QR code ordering. Jerk Jamaican Mountain Grill – Ski-on, ski- off. Open Thursday-Monday from 11:00 a.m. - 3:00 p.m. weather permitting. Ledgewood Yurt – Ski-on, ski-off. Open Thur. - Mon. from 10:30 a.m. to 3:00 p.m. Reservations recommended, walk-ins accepted. Closing for the season after Sunday, March 22nd. Mountain services are subject to change. Please check here daily before making your lunch and lodge choices. Winter Lift Tickets and rentals must be purchased online at this link . Check to see if your pass is good to go using our Online Pass Checker . Find Season Pass FAQs by clicking here . Get your Unlimited Winter Season Pass today! Buy now . Have a ticketing question? Our text number ( 802-523-2202 ) is the best way to get it answered quickly, but please note that we cannot process transactions via text. Have a season pass question? Call 1-800-621-MTNS or email seasonpass@killington.com Please visit Killington.com/cancellation for more details on our cancellation policy. Killington Sports at Route 4 is open Sunday - Thursday from 10:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m., Friday and Saturday from 10:00 a.m. - 6:00 p.m., and 10:00 a.m. - 4:00 p.m. on holidays. Killington is a cashless resort. We accept all major credit cards as well as Apple, Google, and Samsung Pay. *No remaining Peak Dates during winter 2025/26. We strive to have the most up-to-date and accurate snow report, but errors can happen from time to time. If you have feedback or a correction for us, please share it here.

copper mountain.note: Good morning, Copper Mountain athletes! Don’t forget your sunscreen—today brings sunny skies with a high near 58°F. Expect a light breeze around 10 mph this morning, building to about 20 mph this afternoon with gusts into the mid-30s. Snow conditions will continue to change quickly throughout the day, with increasing ground hazards and sticky snow. Stay safe out there, and as always, have fun!

snowbird.note: What’s up party people? This is Otto with your snow report for Saturday, March 21. The skiing was absolutely incredible yesterday and today I’m expecting more of the same. It will be sunny with temps climbing into the mid 60s. The winds should be light and temperatures are already in the high 40s at the summit this morning, so grab your party shirts and a lot of sunscreen, because it won’t take long for the whole mountain to be in perfect spring slush. Also, it’s day 2 of Subaru WinterFest and the party is heating up as fast as the snow. We’ve got DJ’s down in Gad Valley from 9-2 and then Simba Sax and Artikal Sound System jamming on the Plaza Deck from 1:30-5. Make sure to swing by both areas throughout the day to grab some free coffee, snacks, swag, demos and more. Reminder that ALL uphill travel is closed. Have an awesome day out there. Be smart, have fun and get that goggle tan going.
